subject: Replace exFAT with version supporting symlinks.
author: Sorgelig
provenance_doc_ref: patch-provenance.md §3.8 (Class G) discusses the v5.15 equivalents (8b6b8c2f5, df35bdb27)

impact.effect_if_absent: Symlinks on /media/fat (the exFAT data partition) are not readable. MiSTer's arcade organizer builds its entire _Organized tree via symlinks; removing this commit without a replacement silently breaks the organizer. This is a feature loss, not a loud build error.

userspace_coupling.interface: exfat filesystem symlink support (readlink, mkdir/create operations on symlinks)
userspace_coupling.main_mister_ref: Main_MiSTer file_io.cpp:1592 relies on symlinks in _Organized arcade filesystem

forward_port.conflicts: No direct forward-port needed; 6.18 uses vanilla mainline exfat + our 0031 patch (0031-exfat-samsung-symlinks.patch) for Samsung-compatible symlink support per ADR 0010 + ADR 0019

superseded_by: df35bdb27 (Add exFAT with symlinks support, v5.15)

license_provenance.author: Samsung; re-integrated by Sorgelig into Linux-Kernel_MiSTer

notes: This is the v5.13.12-branch precursor to the v5.15 split commits (8b6b8c2f5 'Remove original exFAT driver' + df35bdb27 'Add exFAT with symlinks support'). It is NOT part of the stock v5.15 kernel or our current 6.18 build. All the work here—replacing mainline exfat with Samsung's out-of-tree driver—was done in a single large all-in-one commit on the old v5.13.12 branch (2021-08-25), then reorganized in v5.15 (2021-08-30) as two separate commits for cleaner history. In 6.18, we have DROPPED the out-of-tree driver entirely (per ADR 0010) and instead use vanilla mainline exfat + our carried patch 0031 (0031-exfat-samsung-symlinks.patch, per ADR 0019 option (c)) to restore Samsung-compatible symlink format support. The symlink feature is still available to MiSTer; dropping this old driver did not regress the user experience. This record is filed for historical traceability only; no action is needed for this commit itself.
